name: James Hoffmann's V60 Method
description: The 'Ultimate V60 Technique' by 2007 World Barista Champion James Hoffmann. A two-pour method with a bloom, emphasizing high temperature and gentle agitation for a clean, sweet cup.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.instruction: Pour twice the dose weight in water. Spiral pour from the centre outwards. Swirl the brewer until the mixture is even. Let bloom.

steps.label: First pour
steps.instruction: Pour slowly from centre outwards and then concentrically.

steps.label: Second pour
steps.instruction: Pour concentrically until you reach the total. Give the slurry a gentle swirl.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.instruction: Wait until the coffee has fully drained. Gently stir in both directions, then swirl the brewer to flatten the bed.

name: Tetsu Kasuya's 4:6 Method
description: The 4:6 method by 2016 World Brewers Cup Champion Tetsu Kasuya. Splits the brew water into a 40/60 ratio across five pours. The first two pours control sweetness vs. acidity; the last three control strength. This balanced variant uses equal first pours and two final pours for medium strength.

steps.label: First pour
steps.instruction: Spiral pour from the centre outwards and back to the centre to wet all the coffee. Wait until the water drains.

steps.label: Second pour
steps.instruction: Spiral pour from the centre outwards and back to the centre. Wait until the water drains.

steps.label: Third pour
steps.instruction: Spiral pour from the centre outwards and back to the centre. Wait until the water drains.

steps.label: Fourth pour
steps.instruction: Final pour to reach the total water amount. Wait until the water drains.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.instruction: Wait until the coffee has fully drained.

name: Scott Rao's V60 Method
description: Coffee consultant and author Scott Rao's V60 technique, famous for the 'Rao Spin' — a gentle swirl of the brewer that levels the coffee bed for a more even extraction. Uses a single main pour after the bloom for simplicity.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.instruction: Pour three times the coffee dose weight in water. Stir gently with a spoon to make sure all grounds are wet.

steps.label: Main pour
steps.instruction: Pour in concentric circles until you reach the total water weight.

steps.label: Stir & spin
steps.instruction: Gently stir to knock any grounds clinging to the filter walls. Then give the V60 a gentle swirl — the 'Rao Spin' — to flatten the coffee bed.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.instruction: Wait for the coffee to fully drain. Target a flat, even bed of grounds at the bottom.

name: James Hoffmann's Chemex Method
description: Hoffmann's Chemex technique, closely mirroring his V60 method but adapted for the thicker Chemex filter paper and larger batch size. Produces a very clean, bright cup.

steps.label: Bloom
steps.instruction: Pour water in the center, spiraling outwards. Stir to wet all grounds. Let bloom.

steps.label: First pour
steps.instruction: Pour in a circular motion, breaking up any clumps.

steps.label: Second pour
steps.instruction: Continue pouring in circles until you reach the total water weight.

steps.label: Stir & settle
steps.instruction: Stir once clockwise, then counter-clockwise. Give the Chemex a gentle swirl.

steps.label: Drawdown
steps.instruction: Wait for the drawdown to complete. Total brew time should be around 4 minutes.

name: Alan Adler's Original AeroPress
description: The original recipe from AeroPress inventor Alan Adler, included in the AeroPress packaging. A quick, simple method using lower temperature water and a fine grind that produces a smooth concentrate. Dilute with hot water for an Americano or add milk for a latte.

steps.label: Pour
steps.instruction: Pour hot water into the chamber to level 1 on the AeroPress.

steps.label: Stir
steps.instruction: Stir for about 10 seconds to make sure all grounds are fully saturated.

steps.label: Press
steps.instruction: Insert the plunger and press gently. Pause when you feel resistance, then continue until the plunger reaches the grounds.

steps.label: Dilute
steps.instruction: This is a concentrate. Add hot water to taste for an Americano, or milk for a latte.

name: James Hoffmann's Ultimate AeroPress
description: Hoffmann's 'Ultimate AeroPress Technique' — a surprisingly simple recipe. Standard (non-inverted) position, no preheating, no filter rinsing. A gentle swirl replaces stirring. Light and sweet result, great starting point for experimentation.

steps.label: Pour
steps.instruction: Pour all the water in one go, aiming to wet all the coffee.

steps.label: Steep
steps.instruction: Immediately place the plunger about 1 cm into the brewer to create a vacuum seal. This stops the coffee from dripping through. Wait 2 minutes.

steps.label: Swirl
steps.instruction: Holding the brewer and plunger together, gently swirl the brewer to knock the grounds off the surface.

steps.label: Settle
steps.instruction: Wait 30 seconds for the grounds to sink to the bottom.

steps.label: Press
steps.instruction: Press gently all the way. You can pause when you hit resistance, then continue.

name: James Hoffmann's French Press
description: Hoffmann's French Press technique produces a cleaner, less silty cup than the traditional method. The key: after steeping, you break the crust, skim the foam, then wait for the grounds to sink before pouring. Don't plunge all the way down.

steps.label: Pour
steps.instruction: Pour all the water over the grounds.

steps.label: Steep
steps.instruction: Let it steep undisturbed with the lid off. Don't stir, don't touch it.

steps.label: Break & skim
steps.instruction: Stir the crust on top with a spoon to break it. Then use two spoons to scoop off any foam and floating grounds.

steps.label: Settle
steps.instruction: Let the coffee sit for 5 minutes. The remaining grounds will slowly sink to the bottom.

steps.label: Plunge & pour
steps.instruction: Place the plunger just above the surface of the liquid. Do not press down to the bottom — just use it as a strainer. Pour gently.

name: Wuggy's French Press
description: A straightforward French Press method. Full immersion with a long settle time for a clean cup. Similar in spirit to Hoffmann's approach but with a quicker crust break and longer final rest.

steps.label: Pour
steps.instruction: Pour all the water over the grounds.

steps.label: Steep
steps.instruction: Do nothing. Let it sit with the lid off.

steps.label: Break crust
steps.instruction: Break the crust with a plunger, gently. Don't plunge all the way.

steps.label: Wait
steps.instruction: Let the coffee settle.

steps.label: Plunge & pour
steps.instruction: Press the plunger gently and slowly.
